articles.title: CM Bhajan Lal Sharma Participates in Gram Vikas Chaupal Program
articles.description: CM Bhajan Lal Sharma Participates in Gram Vikas Chaupal Program

articles.title: Election Commission of India announces Rajya Sabha polls for 24 seats on June 18
articles.description: ECI announces Rajya Sabha elections for 24 seats across 10 states, scheduled for June 18, 2026.

articles.title: 'Quran mandates Qurbani': Humayun Kabir stirs row after Bengal cattle slaughter curb
articles.description: A political storm erupted in West Bengal as AJUP chief Humayun Kabir vowed to conduct cow slaughter for Eid, defying state restrictions. He insisted the Quran mandates it, challenging the government's notice. BJP countered, emphasizing law enforcement and opposing illegal slaughter, while some Muslim leaders urged respect for cows.

articles.title: Noa Avishag Schnall: “It’s important to focus on the Palestinians”
articles.description: At the Jaipur Literature Festival, the author, who was among the 428 activists on the Gaza aid flotilla detained and subsequently released by Israel earlier this week, spoke about being an anti-Zionist Arab Jew and about her belief in a free Palestine

articles.title: OPINION | Green Energy Versus Sacred Ecology: The battle over Rajasthan’s Orans
articles.description: Solar expansion in Rajasthan is threatening sacred Orans and local ecosystems. Courts and communities are demanding stronger protection for traditional commons and environmentally sensitive desert landscapes

articles.title: Congress leader Thiruvanchoor Radhakrishnan elected Kerala Speaker
articles.description: Thiruvanchoor Radhakrishnan elected Kerala Speaker, winning 101 votes amid bipartisan support and highlighting his extensive political experience.

articles.title: UP Politics: Abbas Ansari Likely to Join ‘Cockroach Party’, Political Speculation Intensifies
articles.description: UP Politics: Abbas Ansari Likely to Join ‘Cockroach Party’, Political Speculation Intensifies

articles.title: Senior Congress leader Thiruvanchoor Radhakrishnan elected Kerala Assembly Speaker
articles.description: Thiruvanchoor Radhakrishnan has been elected Speaker of the 16th Kerala Legislative Assembly. He secured 101 votes. A C Moideen received 35 votes and B B Gopakumar got 3 votes. The Congress-led UDF holds a majority in the 140-seat Assembly. The opposition fronts announced their nominees earlier.

articles.title: Mamata Banerjee Calls Meet With Councillors After KMC Notices To Abhishek Banerjee Properties
articles.description: The KMC earlier this week initiated a probe into several properties reportedly owned or co-owned by Abhishek Banerjee.

articles.title: Iran condemns 'illegal' US sanctions on ambassador-designate to Lebanon
articles.description: Iran's Foreign Ministry condemned US sanctions on its ambassador-designate to Lebanon, Mohammad Reza Rauf Sheibani, calling the move 'illegal' and a violation of international law. The US sanctioned nine people for enabling Hezbollah.

articles.title: 'We Were Sworn Enemies': Samarth's Words From Wedding Video Take Dark Turn After Twisha's Death
articles.description: Twisha Sharma's tragic death has ignited a contentious dowry case between her family and that of her husband, Samarth Singh, following a seemingly joyful wedding captured in a cinematic video.

articles.title: Live: US Will Seize, Destroy Uranium, Says Trump; Iran Deal Hopes Dim Over Hormuz
articles.description: US President Trump indicated willingness to allow some highly enriched uranium under Iranian sites to remain but insisted the US will take control and likely destroy it.

articles.title: Muslim Side Moves Supreme Court, Challenges HC Order In Bhojshala Complex Case
articles.description: After filing the petition, the Muslim side expressed hope that the apex court would stay the Madhya Pradesh HC ruling, which quashed the 2003 ASI order

articles.title: Ebola outbreak: Flights diverted, borders tightened — how countries are taking precautions
articles.description: In response to a severe Ebola outbreak, countries worldwide are tightening travel restrictions. The US has implemented mandatory screenings for incoming passengers at Washington Dulles from areas affected by the outbreak, while Uganda has halted transportation connections with the Democratic Republic of Congo.

articles.title: Iran says latest US proposal has ‘narrowed gaps’: Report
articles.description: Iran says latest US proposal has ‘narrowed gaps’: Report

articles.title: Iran digs in as Trump warns military strikes could return ‘very quickly’ amid stalled peace talks
articles.description: Trump also faces domestic pressure ahead of November’s midterm elections, with his approval rating near its lowest since he returned to the White House.

articles.title: NEET paper not leaked, only some questions came out: NTA chief tells Parliament committee
articles.description: NTA Chief Abhishek Singh and Higher Education Secretary Vineet Joshi informed the panel that malpractices and irregularities had been detected but did not amount to a complete paper leak

articles.title: Uttar Pradesh to introduce uniform dress code in all colleges, universities: 'To promote equality, reduce discrimination'
articles.description: The proposal was put forward by Governor Anandiben Patel during a review meeting held at Lok Bhavan on Thursday.

articles.title: NEET-UG paper leak: Parliamentary panel grills NTA chief on probe
articles.description: A Parliamentay panel grilled NTA DG Abhishek Singh on the NEET-UG paper leak. Singh informed them the leak wasn't from NTA's system and the CBI is probing the matter. The re-examination is scheduled for June 21.

articles.title: US Pauses $14B Taiwan Arms Sale Over Iran Conflict Munitions Needs
articles.description: The US has paused a $14B arms sale to Taiwan to ensure munitions for its Iran conflict operations. While officials cite stockpile needs, President Trump hinted it could be a 'bargaining chip' with China, as Taiwan urges the sale to continue.

articles.title: Chandigarh: Sanitation workers call off strike, demands to be considered in a month, says Cheema
articles.description: Sanitation workers were on strike for the past two weeks seeking regularisation of services of all outsourced and contractual staff, restoration of old pension scheme and a hike in monthly wages of contractual workers

articles.title: US and Iran still at odds on key issues, but both sides report signs of progress
articles.description: US President Donald Trump said the US will eventually recover Iran's stockpile of highly enriched uranium.

articles.title: The burden of borrowed expectations
articles.description: Somewhere between childhood and adulthood, an unannounced transfer occurs. A parent’s unspoken fear becomes our ambition, a family’s definition of what counts as a life well lived becomes our standard.

articles.title: US missile stockpile hit after defending Israel during Iran conflict: Report
articles.description: Washington launched more than 200 Terminal High Altitude Area Defense (THAAD) interceptors to help defend Israel, along with over 100 Standard Missile-3 and Standard Missile-6 interceptors fired from naval vessels in the eastern Mediterranean.

articles.title: Indian Army's Special Forces make historic debut at SOF Week in Florida
articles.description: An Indian Armed Forces delegation led by Lt Gen Pushpendra Pal Singh participated in SOF Week 2026 in Tampa, Florida. In a landmark first, the Indian Army's Special Forces debuted at the CAPEX Capability Demonstration, showcasing their operational skills.

articles.title: Denied relief, Umar Khalid moved high court for interim bail
articles.description: The petition, scheduled to be heard on Friday by a bench of justices Prathiba M Singh and Madhu Jain, challenges the trial court’s May 19 order refusing him 15 days’ interim bail

articles.title: Trump Says Sending 5,000 US Troops To Poland
articles.description: US President Donald Trump said Thursday he was sending 5,000 troops to Poland.

articles.title: Voter confusion fears over simultaneous polls ‘misplaced’, says ONOE JPC chairman
articles.description: JPC chairman P.P. Chaudhary defends simultaneous polls, asserting voters can distinguish national and local issues effectively.

articles.title: Kerala to scrap ’23 land act, set up vigilance panel headed by judge
articles.description: Kerala to scrap ’23 land act, set up vigilance panel headed by judge

articles.description: Ebola outbreak forces India to put off Africa summit

articles.description: Nagpur: The Nagpur bench of Bombay High Court recently ruled that asking a working wife to contribute her salary toward household expenses \"cannot, in.

articles.title: Women sanitary workers meet mayor, seek protection
articles.description: Nagpur: Growing concerns over the safety of women sanitary workers in Nagpur came to the forefront on Thursday after women sanitation employees from P.

articles.title: PCMC, MNGL & co-operatives dept meet societies’ representatives to promote PNG use, discuss other issues
articles.description: PCMC, MNGL & co-operatives dept meet societies’ representatives to promote PNG use, discuss other issues

articles.title: Foreign passport-holding Lankan Tamil arrested for illegal voting in Tamil Nadu elections
articles.description: Foreign passport-holding Lankan Tamil arrested for illegal voting in Tamil Nadu elections

articles.title: Chinese Proverb of the Day: Never does a woman lie in a more cunning way than... — Life lessons on perception, manipulation, suspicion and why trust takes years to build and seconds to break
articles.description: Chinese Proverb of the Day highlights the meaning of “Never does a woman lie in a more cunning way than when she tells the truth to someone who doesnt believe her.” The saying reflects how trust and perception strongly influence human communication and relationships. It suggests that when someone has already decided not to believe another person, even honest words can appear manipulative or deceptive.

articles.title: 'Using Nuclear Weapons Only...': Putin's Address To Nation After Deploying Nukes Near NATO Border
articles.description: As massive Russia-Belarus nuclear drills wrapped up, Vladimir Putin declared that nuclear weapons remain an “exceptional measure” - but warned they are still a critical guarantee for the security of the Russia-Belarus Union State amid rising global tensions.

articles.title: Tamil Nadu school topper’s family struggle to come to terms with boy’s death before results
articles.description: 15-year-old Abishek, Class 10 topper at Kavarapettai Govt High School with 495/500, died drowning after his last exam in Tiruvallur.

articles.title: West’s power crumbling? Putin’s top minister Lavrov says US empire ‘fears competition'
articles.description: Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov launched a fierce attack on the West, accusing the U.S. and its allies of using sanctions, seizures and “invasions” out of fear of losing global dominance. Speaking in Moscow, Lavrov claimed the Western-led system no longer guarantees profits or control, forcing Washington into “dishonest competition” against rivals like Russia and China. The remarks came as Donald Trump weighs renewed military pressure on Iran after stalled ceasefire talks. Lavrov also pushed for a “multipolar world,” warning against Western “dictatorship” while backing closer Russia-China co-ordination through BRICS and the SCO.

articles.title: Manipur DGP moved to Cabinet Secretariat
articles.description: Rajiv Singh appointed Secretary (Security) in Cabinet Secretariat after serving as Manipur DGP during recent ethnic violence.

articles.title: BJP functionary’s request to HR&CE Minister
articles.description: BJP's Vinoj Selvam urges HR&CE Minister Ramesh to worship solely in Srirangam temple during his visits.

articles.title: Leaders of various political parties in Puducherry pay tributes to former PM Rajiv Gandhi
articles.description: Tributes paid to former PM Rajiv Gandhi on his death anniversary, led by Lieutenant Governor K. Kailashnathan in Puducherry.

articles.title: 'Hang him to death': Seer slams AJUP chief over ritual sacrifice row
articles.description: Jagadguru Swami Shri Satishacharya Ji Maharaj condemned AJUP chief Humayun Kabir's remarks on ritual sacrifice in West Bengal, demanding the death penalty for him. The seer warned against cow slaughter, while Kabir insisted qurbani would continue.

articles.title: '$1.2 trillion stolen...': Sen. Kennedy REVEALS SHOCKING details of California Medicaid fraud
articles.description: Sen. John Kennedy (R-La.) explained how bad policies in states such as California and Minnesota have contributed to pervasive Medicaid fraud in a speech on the U.S. Senate floor.

articles.title: India-Africa Summit postponed due to Ebola outbreak, MEA announces
articles.description: On Africa Day 2026, Minister Kirti Vardhan Singh hailed India-Africa ties. Meanwhile, the MEA postponed the Fourth India-Africa Forum Summit in New Delhi due to an Ebola outbreak in Africa, designated a PHEIC by the WHO.

articles.title: NEET leak: Rahul Gandhi backs protests, demands Pradhan's resignation
articles.description: Rahul Gandhi backed Youth Congress protests over the NEET UG paper leak, questioning PM Modi's silence and demanding Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation. Protests erupted in Jaipur and Bengaluru, with police using force on agitators.

articles.title: Pakistan accused of enabling repression against Ahmadiyya community
articles.description: Amnesty International's open letter accuses Pakistani authorities of enabling 'structural discrimination' against the Ahmadiyya community, citing harassment, disruption of worship, and calling for immediate measures to protect their rights.

articles.title: Ladakh: Passing out parade held for promotional courses at PTC Stongsar
articles.description: DGP Ladakh Mukesh Singh attended the passing out parade for 115 police personnel who completed Pre-Promotional Courses at Police Training Centre Stongsar in Leh. The 50-day training aimed to enhance professional competence and operational efficiency.

articles.title: France condemns Israel as it deports 37 French nationals over Gaza flotilla
articles.description: France said Israel is deporting 37 French nationals detained aboard a Gaza-bound aid flotilla to Turkey, as Paris summoned a senior Israeli diplomat over a video showing Israel's national security minister taunting the activists. France said it was too early to discuss sanctions against the minister.

articles.title: Set up new CBI team to probe RG Kar case, submit report by June 25: Calcutta HC
articles.description: A three-member Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) special investigation team (SIT), headed by a joint director (Eastern Zone), to probe 2024 RG Kar Hospital rape and murder case: HC

articles.title: High Court stays Himachal government notification exempting Vigilance Bureau from RTI Act
articles.description: Plea challenged March 12 notification, says exemption violated RTI provisions in corruption-related cases
